falsification_conditions.condition: Price closes above the 200-day moving average by 5% or more
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: A strong bullish breakout would contradict the 'HOLD' signal and indicate a trend reversal, invalidating the bottom pattern assumption.

falsification_conditions.condition: Blowoff score exceeds 60 for 3 consecutive days
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: A significant increase in blowoff momentum would refute the current 'HOLD' by showing explosive upward movement inconsistent with a bottom pattern.

falsification_conditions.condition: The S&P 500 futures gap up by 2% or more on a major economic report day (e.g., Non-Farm Payrolls)
falsification_conditions.what_it_would_mean: A violent market reaction to positive data would disprove the 'UNKNOWN' verdict by demonstrating clear bullish conviction contrary to the bottom pattern.

doctrine: If you can't list what would prove your call wrong, you don't have a real call.
